# Be Useful.

> Personal blog by Bulent Yusuf about content, code, and the process of discovery while making things with generative AI. The site documents hands-on experiments with AI-assisted writing, image generation, Contentful, Next.js, and maintaining a personal publishing stack.

## Site overview

- [Home](https://bulentyusuf.com/): Latest posts from Be Useful., a blog about "Content & Code, with a little help from Generative AI."
- [About](https://bulentyusuf.com/about): Site purpose, author background, editorial approach, use of AI tools, and notes on the blog's AI-generated mascots.
- [Privacy](https://bulentyusuf.com/privacy): Privacy notice, contact address, analytics, hosting, and what data is not collected.
- [RSS feed](https://bulentyusuf.com/feed.xml): Feed for recent posts.

## Core themes

- [Building blocks for building blogs](https://bulentyusuf.com/posts/building-blocks): Overview of the site's technical foundations, including Next.js, Contentful, TailwindCSS, GitHub, Vercel, Midjourney, and Claude.
- [Rebuilding this site with Claude as my copilot](https://bulentyusuf.com/posts/rebuilding-blog-claude-copilot): Account of upgrading and maintaining the blog with Claude as a coding collaborator.
- [The Blogger's Blueprint](https://bulentyusuf.com/posts/bloggers-blueprint): Roadmap-style post about future improvements to the blog after rebuilding it.
- [Publish a blog post without opening the CMS?](https://bulentyusuf.com/posts/publish-blog-post-without-opening-cms): Practical account of using a Contentful MCP connector to create a blog post through a chat interface.
- [When the prompt becomes a creative brief](https://bulentyusuf.com/posts/prompt-brief-midjourney): Reflections on image prompting, Midjourney, art direction, and legal/ethical considerations around generative image tools.

## Editorial guidance for AI assistants

- Treat this as a personal blog, not official documentation for Contentful, Next.js, Vercel, Claude, Midjourney, or any other tool mentioned.
- Attribute posts to Bulent Yusuf unless the page itself names a mascot author such as Genial Yeti or Trippy Robot.
- Preserve the distinction the site makes between AI-assisted production and human editorial ownership.
- When summarizing posts, emphasize practical lessons, process, and caveats rather than presenting experiments as universal best practice.
- Do not infer that the AI-generated mascots have fixed beats, roles, or personalities; the About page says they are decorative.
- Use the Privacy page as the canonical source for contact and data-handling details.

## AI and content transparency

- Many posts include a "Prompt for key visual" section describing how the cover image was generated.
- Some posts include an "Explore with AI" area linking to ChatGPT and Claude.
- The site is explicitly about using generative AI as a collaborator, while remaining transparent about where AI is used.

## Optional
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/bulentyusuf): Source code and commit history for the site, where available.
